title: 10 best herbal capsules for gum infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The herb's antibacterial agents also inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to protect against further damage and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.

Antimicrobial agents: The herb has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and fungi, including those commonly responsible for gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans, helping to combat infection and promote healing.

Wound-healing compounds: Pot marigold contains triterpenoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have been found to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood circulation, and enhancing the growth of new tissue, which can help repair damaged gum tissue.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, thereby aiding in the treatment of gum infections.

Flavonoids (especially triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids): These compounds possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can help combat the growth of bacteria and other pathogens responsible for gum infections, promoting healing and recovery.

Tricyclene and sesquiterpenes (especially Calendulin): These terpenoid compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing gum inflammation, preventing the spread of infection, and promoting the recovery of gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria in gum infections.
Leaves: They possess antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help in preventing the spread of infection and promoting healing in the gums.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing gum inflammation and preventing infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom to obtain the optimal medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with a small amount of carrier starch like cornstarch in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a small spoon for precise measurement.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial properties, which can effectively combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The bioactive compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms associated with gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms such as swelling, pain, and bleeding, making it a natural and effective remedy for gum infection treatment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage capsules contain antimicrobial compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in sage capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection.
Antioxidant properties: Sage capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, protecting the gums from damage caused by oxidative stress and promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection and reduce inflammation in gum infections.

Ursolic acid: A triterpene that has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections and reduce inflammation.

Salvianolic acid B: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum infections, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections in the gums.
Stems: They are used because they contain tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in gum infections.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which contribute to gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at the peak of flowering to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to prevent mold and mildew.
Grind the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the powdered material with a gelatinous capsule filler in a 1:1 ratio to create a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with gum infection because they contain extracts of Echinacea angustifolia, a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and discomfort in the gums. The capsule's antibacterial properties also combat the bacteria responsible for gum disease, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's immunomodulatory effects stimulate the body's natural defense against infection, allowing the gums to heal faster and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: The compounds present in Echinacea capsules, such as cinnamic acid and echinacein, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, including those that cause gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's active ingredients, like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and redness associated with gum inflammation, making it easier to manage symptoms of gum infection.

Immune system support: Echinacea is known for its immunomodulatory effects, which can enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms against infections, including those affecting the gums, by stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating immune responses.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in gum infections.

Alkaloids (including Cichoric Acid and Alkylamides): These compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to stimulate the immune system and prevent the spread of infection in the gums.

Polysaccharides (including Echinacoside and Echinacin): These compounds have been found to have prebiotic properties, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help to prevent the imbalance of oral flora that can lead to gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils and phenolic acids with antimicrobial properties to help combat gum infection.
Seeds: Have saponins that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects to aid in gum infection treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate them in your garden.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 200 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of glycerin in a large bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 50 ml of distilled water to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Fill 100 vegetarian capsules with 400 mg of the prepared mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with gum infection because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that combat swelling and reduce pain. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum disease. Additionally, maidenhair tree has antibacterial compounds that directly target and eliminate harmful microorganisms responsible for gum infections. By reducing inflammation and eliminating bacterial growth, maidenhair tree capsules provide natural relief from gum infection symptoms and promote a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_properties: Tea tree oil: Has antibacterial properties that can help combat bacteria that cause gum infections, such as periodontitis.
Myrrh: Has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce swelling and prevent further infection of the gums.
Goldenseal: Contains berberine, which has antimicrobial properties that may help combat gum infections caused by bacteria.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the progression of gum infection.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been found to have antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to gum disease.
Ginkgolides: These diterpene trilactones have been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help treat gum infections.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 30-40 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of gelatin or starch powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin or starch capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler machine and press the capsules.
Store the ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container and consume 120-240 mg per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has antibacterial activity that combats the underlying cause of gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and promote tissue healing, making it an effective natural remedy for treating gum infection. This natural approach can be particularly beneficial for individuals who are looking for a non-invasive and holistic solution.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Curcumin, a key compound found in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation in the gums, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling associated with gum infections.
Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric's antimicrobial agents, including curcumin and other bioactive molecules, help combat bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing their growth and spread.

Wound-healing properties: Turmeric contains compounds that promote collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and enhance wound healing, which can aid in the repair of damaged gums and tissues infected with bacteria.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Tumerones (sesquiterpenoids): Tumerones exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum infections, thereby promoting oral health.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of gum infections and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most used part, as it contains high levels of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation.
Roots: Used for their antibacterial properties, which help combat the bacterial infections that cause gum disease.
Seeds: Employed for their antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and promote overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried turmeric root powder for the capsule filling.
Mix 500 grams of turmeric root powder with 200 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into empty vegetarian capsules at 500 milligrams each.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to two years.
Label the containers with the name turmeric root capsules and the dosage of 2-3 capsules per day.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make capsules for gum infection as they contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has antimicrobial properties.
Buds: Used to make capsules for gum infection as they contain oils that help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections.

remedy_recipe: Crush 500 grams of syzygium aromaticum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 250 grams of gelatin and 250 grams of cornstarch in a large bowl.
Add 500 milliliters of hot water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin dissolves completely in 10 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 1000 gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture in 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers according to the desired quantity and quality.

Dry the cleaned plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Use a coffee grinder to grind the dried plant material into a fine powder with 1 teaspoon dosage.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in aloe vera capsules, like saponins and phenolic acids, can combat bacterial infections that cause periodontitis and other gum diseases, preventing their spread.

remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from mature leaves after 6-8 weeks of growth and 3-4 weeks of drying.
Dry the gel in a single layer at 35-40°c for 3-5 days to remove excess moisture completely.
Weigh 100-200 grams of dried aloe vera gel and grind it into fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 500-700 mg of the powder into vegetarian capsules using an encapsulation machine.
Label and date the capsules properly and store them in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems according to good agricultural practices and dry them properly within 24 hours.
Combine dried gaultheria procumbens with 100 milligrams of powdered vitamin c and 50 milliliters of glycerin in a glass jar.
Store the mixture for two weeks to allow for a minimum of 30 days of fermentation time allowing optimal release of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids transferring the liquid to clean glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Fill gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the strained liquid per capsule and seal them immediately for optimal potency and shelf life.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacteria in gum infections.
Barks: The barks of the yarrow plant are rich in tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and protect the gums from infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of boiling water with 50 grams of the powdered herb and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.
Fill #00 size gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the cooled liquid extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage capsules helps with gum infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as boric acid and lignans, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, borage contains antioxidants that help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, borage capsules may help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as pain, swelling, and redness, promoting healthy gums and a reduced risk of complications like tooth loss.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Borage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) and sesquiterpenes, which can reduce swelling and pain in the gums, helping to alleviate symptoms of gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in borage, such as beta-sitosterol, have been shown to possess antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans.

Wound healing enhancement: Borage's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may also enhance wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing scar formation, which can aid in the recovery from gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the borage plant contain bioactive compounds like glycosides and saponins, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat gum infections.
Seeds: Borage seeds are rich in omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can reduce inflammation and promote healing of infected gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality borago officinalis flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material in a warm oven at 35°c for 30 minutes or air-dry for several days.
Grind the dried herbal mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered borago officinalis with 500 milliliters of glycerin in a sterile environment.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium capsules helps with gum infection because they contain the herb Geranium maculatum, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsule's active compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause infections. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of wild geranium may help to promote healthy gum tissue and prevent further infection.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The tannins present in Wild Geranium help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, which can aid in reducing inflammation and preventing further bacterial growth in the gums.

Antimicrobial properties: Wild Geranium has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activity against various microorganisms that can cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans, helping to combat the underlying infection.

Wound-healing properties: The saponins in Wild Geranium capsules may help to promote collagen synthesis and improve tissue strength, which can aid in the healing process of inflamed or infected gums, reducing the risk of complications and promoting oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in gums infected with bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antimicrobial properties, the rhyzomes effectively combat bacterial growth and prevent further infection in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of fresh geranium maculatum leaves and stems.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with a mixture of 250 milligrams powdered herb and 500 milligrams silica gel.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with gum infection because they contain the extract of Eucalyptus globulus, a natural antiseptic that effectively tackles bacterial buildup in the mouth. The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the gums, reducing swelling and redness caused by infection. Additionally, its antimicrobial agents combat harmful bacteria, promoting healthy oral tissue and preventing further infection. This holistic approach to gum care provides long-term relief from gum infections, promoting overall oral health and wellbeing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules contain eucalyptol, a natural compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi responsible for gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' active compounds, such as cineole and globulol, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting healing and reducing the severity of gum infections.

Antioxidant activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antioxidant properties may help protect the gums from oxidative stress caused by inflammation and bacterial damage, thereby supporting overall oral health and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain phenolic acids and tannins, which exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria that cause gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning hours after dew has evaporated.
Dry the fresh leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6 hours or air dry them for 7 days.
Grind 30g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve.
Mix the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 200g of potato starch in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300mg of the blend and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gum infection are a natural remedy that helps to treat and prevent gum infection by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healing. The best herbal capsules for gum infection include Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Salvia officinalis, Syzygium aromaticum, Commiphora molmol, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Hydrastis canadensis, and Origanum vulgare. If left untreated, gum infection can lead to tooth loss, bad breath, and even heart problems, negatively impacting one's quality of life, self-esteem, and overall health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal capsules are various oral health issues, such as bad breath, toothache, and mouth ulcers. Herbal capsules like Neem, Turmeric, and Clove extracts are commonly used to combat these problems. These natural remedies help to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing in the gums and oral tissues, providing a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with gum infection is a blend of Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Clove. Echinacea enhances the immune system, while Goldenseal's berberine reduces inflammation and fights off bacteria. Clove's eugenol has antimicrobial properties that combat infection and promote healing. This combination helps to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial growth, and promote gum tissue repair. It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal supplements, especially if you have a severe infection or underlying medical conditions.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal capsules can be beneficial in treating gum infections due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce dental plaque formation. This study, which reviewed clinical trials on the effectiveness of herbal products in gingivitis, found that various herbal medicines, including pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, were effective in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding. As a result, herbal capsules containing these ingredients can be a good option for managing gum infections, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote overall oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, has been shown to effectively combat the bacteria responsible for gum disease, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia capsules promote a healthy oral environment, reducing symptoms of gum infection, including redness, swelling, and pain. Its natural properties also help to soothe and calm the gums, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cymene: This monoterpenoid compound has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for gum infections.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, cineole can reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal gum infections by reducing swelling and preventing bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to create capsules due to their high content of tea tree oil, which is known for its antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infection.
Barks: Utilized in capsules because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing gum inflammation and promoting healing.
Seeds: Incorporated into capsules for their oil, which has been shown to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum infection symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or wild source carefully.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours thoroughly.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle efficiently.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of a natural filler such as tapioca starch to achieve the desired capsule weight accurately.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine quickly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause gum infections. Additionally, Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for the gums to heal. This makes Echinacea purpurea capsules a promising natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation in gum tissue, which is a key factor in gum infections.
Iridoids: They possess antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections in the gums.
Triterpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing gum inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against gum infections.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce gum inflammation and prevent infection.
Flowers: They are rich in alkylamides and polysaccharides, which have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, promoting healing and preventing gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the summer months when in full bloom.
Clean and dry the roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots and flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 1000 rpm.
Fill gelatin capsules with a measured 250 milligram dose of the powdered echinacea purpurea mixture.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in E. globulus, such as eucalyptol and camphor, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, including Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of E. globulus help to reduce swelling and pain, promoting a faster recovery and healing of the infected gum tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Catechin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Flavonoids: They have antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by gum infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which has antimicrobial properties effective against gum infections.
Barks: They are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the gums.
Stems: They are also used due to their essential oil content, which includes compounds like eucalyptol, that help to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial growth in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature trees at dawn when essential oils are concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at 35°c for 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 200 rpm for 2 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of purified water.
Encapsulate the mixture in 500 gelatin capsules at 200 capsules per hour using an automated capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis capsules helps with gum infection because they contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Salvia officinalis, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the mouth. This leads to a reduction in swelling, pain, and bleeding associated with gum infections. Additionally, Salvia officinalis capsules may help to prevent the progression of gum disease by promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing plaque formation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help soothe gum inflammation and prevent the spread of infection.
Salvianolic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce gum inflammation and prevent tissue damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used due to their ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Used due to their ability to combat bacterial growth and reduce pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20g of the dried salvia officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin powder and 50g of water to create a gel-like mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum capsules helps with gum infection because they contain the herb clove, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound eugenol in clove oil has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Syzygium aromaticum capsules can help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, such as pain, swelling, and bad breath. Regular use of these capsules may also promote healthy gum tissue and prevent future infections.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with gum infections.
Caryophyllene: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected gum tissue.

Limonene: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with gum infections, while also promoting wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their antibacterial properties, which help combat gum infection-causing bacteria.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Flowers: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Crush 250mg of dried syzygium aromaticum bark into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the powdered syzygium aromaticum with 25mg of filler material in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill the mixture into size 0 empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.
Label and date the container with the name and dosage of the herbal syzygium aromaticum capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol capsules helps with gum infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains compounds like beta-boswellic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections. Additionally, Commiphora molmol has been traditionally used to treat gum problems and oral infections due to its ability to promote healing and reduce swelling in the gums. This makes it a potential natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Furanosesquiterpenes: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacteria that cause gum infections, such as periodontitis and gingivitis.
Phenolic compounds: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain in the gums, and may also inhibit the growth of oral pathogens.
Alkaloids: These compounds possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the gums from infection and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight infection in the gums.
Buds: Utilized for their antibacterial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections in the gums.
Fruits: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and fighting infection in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried commiphora molmol bark from a trusted source ensuring freshness and quality.
Grind the commiphora molmol bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the ground commiphora molmol powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the commiphora molmol gelatin mixture using a capsule filler for 15 minutes.
Store the filled commiphora molmol capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with gum infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum infections. This helps to prevent the progression of gum disease and reduce symptoms such as swelling, pain, and bleeding. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help to promote healing and prevent further damage to the gums, making it a valuable natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum infections.

Gingerols' breakdown product, Shogaol's breakdown product, or more specifically, 6-gingerol's breakdown product: Gingerdiol: Gingerdiol, has been found to possess antibacterial properties and to be effective in reducing the severity of gum infections by suppressing the growth of bacteria.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make capsules for gum infection because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: Roots are used to make capsules for gum infection because they contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Buds: Buds are used to make capsules for gum infection because they are rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation and fight infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 kg of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes when they are mature and the skin is dry.
Wash the rhizomes under running water to remove impurities and dry them thoroughly with clean towels.
Slice the dried rhizomes into thin pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried rhizome pieces into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 150 rpm for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 250-300 mg of the zingiber officinale powder and seal them with a capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of biofilms that contribute to gum infections. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba capsules improve blood flow to the gums, promoting healing and reducing the severity of gum inflammation. This results in faster recovery and reduced pain, making Ginkgo biloba capsules a promising natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with gum infections.

Flavonoids (specifically Kaempferol and Quercetin): These phenolic compounds have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which may help combat bacterial growth and oxidative stress in gum infections.
Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide B): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent tissue damage in gum infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: They are used due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help combat gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of powdered ginkgo biloba with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of glycerin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains berberine, a compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. By targeting the root cause of the infection, Hydrastis canadensis capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and bleeding. This natural remedy also supports the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the infection and restore oral health.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacteria that cause gum infection, such as Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans.
Hydrastine: Displays anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which aid in reducing swelling and fighting off infection in the gums.
Vasculine (Berbamine): Shows strong antimicrobial properties, which help in eliminating the causative agents of gum infection, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used for Hydrastis canadensis capsules, as it contains the highest concentration of berberine, a key compound for treating gum infections.
Barks: The barks are also used due to their high berberine content, making them a suitable alternative or complementary part for treating gum infections.
Leaves: The leaves contain some berberine, although in lower concentrations than the roots and barks, they can still be used for treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hydrastis canadensis roots by selecting organic and high-quality sources to ensure potency.
Grind 2-4 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1-2 teaspoons of the powdered root with 1-2 teaspoons of a natural binder such as gum arabic for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty capsules with the mixture in small batches for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place and consume 1-2 capsules daily with water after meals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare capsules helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly carvacrol, exhibit broad-spectrum activity against various bacteria, fungi, and viruses that cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and killing the underlying pathogens, Origanum vulgare capsules promote healing and prevent further infection. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to reduce plaque formation and prevent the onset of gum disease, making it a valuable natural remedy for maintaining oral health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties that help combat the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infection.
Carvacrol: Displays anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help reduce swelling and infection in the gums.
Rosmarinic acid: Shows antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in reducing gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly carvacrol, which has antimicrobial properties effective against gum infections.
Stems: The stems contain a smaller amount of essential oils compared to leaves but are still used in some formulations to provide a sustained release of active compounds.

Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of Origanum vulgare contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested origanum vulgare leaves in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried origanum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered origanum vulgare with 2 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the origanum vulgare-gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine.
